affordances.name: Reconstructable decision trail
affordances.mechanism: When advice will be acted on by others or reviewed later, require a reconstructable trail from inputs, assumptions, logical steps, and intentional design choices to the final recommendation and owner action.

use_when: Decisions, outputs, or model behavior must be explained clearly enough that others can inspect how conclusions were reached.
The cost of error is high and the organization needs a recoverable trail from evidence to judgment to action.
Trust depends not only on being right, but on being able to show what happened, why it happened, and who changed what.
do_not_use_when: Traceability would become performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.
The work is being treated as a linear mechanical blueprint even though implementation depends on engagement, accountability, or social-system behavior.
Documentation overhead would lock the team into a recorded path and discourage justified late-stage pivots.
case_evidence_needed: The recommendation, decision, or output that must later be inspected.
The inputs, assumptions, logical steps, and design choices behind it.
The owner of each judgment or change.
The failure, review, or learning moment that the trail must support.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to state what would make the recommendation reconstructable later: inputs, assumptions, reasoning steps, owner changes, and the review question the trace must answer.
evidence_required: The logical steps or design choices.
The actor responsible for changing or approving the path.
good_output_shape: Links evidence to judgment to action.
Names the assumptions that must remain inspectable.
Separates useful traceability from documentation theater.
Defines what a later reviewer should be able to reconstruct.

diagnostic_questions: Could a later reviewer reconstruct how this advice moved from evidence to judgment to action?
Which assumptions and owner changes would disappear if we only kept the final recommendation?
Is this trace improving learning and accountability, or only creating documentation?
misuse_guards: Do not treat documentation volume as proof of judgment quality.
Do not turn traceability into a bureaucratic lock on a path that should pivot.
Do not hide social-system implementation risk behind a neat causal diagram.

source_evidence.source_quote: decisions, outputs, or model behavior must be explained clearly enough that others can inspect how conclusions were reached
show what happened, why it happened, and who changed what

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports one reconstructable trail affordance for inputs, assumptions, logical steps, ownership, and later review.
dropped_material: Did not split Minto Pyramid, logic trees, and AI explainability into separate affordances.
Kept business-case and first-principles material as support for the causal trail.
open_questions: PR40 should test whether this card improves packet handoff without encouraging documentation theater.

absence_records.reason: The source warns that traceability can become performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.

source_evidence.source_quote: traceability becomes performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning

absence_records.reason: The source rejects treating implementation as a purely rational linear process when social-system engagement and accountability determine whether the trace is useful.

source_evidence.source_quote: Treating implementation as a fundamentally rational, linear process ignores that a social system is a living system, not amenable to mechanical blueprints.

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 8 source reading found strong reviewed depth for Auditability Traceability. Extracted one reconstructable decision trail and rejected performative documentation plus linear-blueprint misuse.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether this card helps the next LLM ask for inspectable evidence without over-documenting.
